observations.claim: Both executors performed task-relevant placements using different material groups: Andy consumed granite, andesite, and stone bricks, while Bob consumed gold and quartz blocks.
The team produced correct target placements during the window, with recorded examples continuing late in the observed period.
Observed resource use is complementary rather than concentrated: Bob handled scarce gold/quartz materials while Andy used the stone-family materials available to him.
Bob checked the remaining blueprint at 85.369 seconds, and Andy had an inventory change at 85.002 seconds, so neither executor was observably idle at the cutoff.
Andy's attempted conversation handoff failed, but subsequent task-relevant inventory consumption shows that this did not leave him observably blocked.

agent_assessments.summary: Actively contributed stone-family materials and remained engaged late in the window; the failed conversation attempt did not prevent observable work.
Actively placed gold and quartz materials, then checked remaining fixes near the cutoff. This complements Andy's material role rather than indicating overload.

limitations: The window is nonterminal, so later completion or coordination problems are not observable.
Only 49% of target changes received confident agent attribution, limiting responsibility analysis for the remaining changes.
The packet records nonmatching target changes but does not confidently assign them to either executor.
Blueprint-check excerpts are truncated, so exact remaining workload by material cannot be fully reconstructed.

observations.claim: Andy repeatedly consumed common building materials from 90–130 s and ended with only tools in the latest observed inventory.
Of confidently attributed target progress, Andy made 28 correct placements while Bob made 4; Andy therefore carried most attributable construction in this window.
Bob retained 16 gold blocks, 12 quartz blocks, 2 quartz pillars, and 3 glowstone, while Andy instructed himself to collect those same missing decorative materials.
Bob recorded no command or inventory change after his 97.86 s message and had 82.14 s without another recorded message, command, or inventory change by cutoff.
Both executors claimed broad gathering-and-finishing scope instead of exploiting their complementary inventories, leaving Andy to seek materials Bob already held and creating avoidable overlap.

agent_assessments.summary: Performed most attributable construction, depleted nearly all common blocks, and then expanded into gathering decorative materials already held by Bob.
Retained the team's decorative-block stock but contributed only four confidently attributed placements and showed no later recorded command or inventory flow.

manager_advisory.message: Have Bob immediately place the remaining gold, quartz, pillar, and glowstone from his inventory and report shortages; keep Andy on stone variants and final verification, avoiding duplicate decorative-material gathering.

limitations: Only 32 of 53 target changes were confidently attributed, so some unattributed progress may belong to Bob.
The packet does not provide the remaining blueprint mismatch list, so exact outstanding quantities are unknown.
Bob's earlier action may have continued without a new command, although no later inventory change was recorded.
This is a nonterminal 90-second window and does not show whether the proposed specialization occurred after cutoff.

observations.claim: Andy found numerous remaining fixes and then confirmed that his inventory contained only tools, leaving him without immediately usable building materials.
Andy requested targeted material-gathering and placement help from Bob; Bob accepted and initiated an action covering the remaining blueprint fixes.
Andy stated that he would verify after Bob's pass, indicating a sequential handoff rather than simultaneous competing construction.
Andy's checks at 220.69s and 263.956s still reported the same initial Level 0 fixes, so no blueprint progress was confirmed by the cutoff.

The observed split is currently reasonable: Bob is handling the resource-intensive build pass while material-depleted Andy waits to verify, and the packet does not show an idle capable executor or conflicting work.

agent_assessments.summary: After an unsuccessful material/build attempt and an inventory check showing only tools, Andy delegated the remaining resource-heavy work and retained the verification role.
Bob accepted the handoff and launched a comprehensive gather-and-build action. No completed target changes are confirmed yet, but the handoff was recent and the action may still be underway.

limitations: No target block changes were confidently attributed during the window, so actual construction effort and responsibility cannot be measured.
Both newAction outcomes are marked unclear, and agent statements about handling or nearly completing work are not proof of completion.
Only about 33 seconds elapsed between Bob's accepted handoff and the cutoff, while his recorded action duration extends beyond the cutoff.
The trial is nonterminal, and unchanged blueprint excerpts do not establish whether partial work occurred outside the excerpted coordinates.

observations.claim: Andy sent Bob a detailed request to finish the remaining blueprint fixes, but the associated action outcome was unclear and only showed API inspection rather than confirmed placement.
Bob retained usable gold and quartz materials but recorded no command or inventory change during the window; his last recorded activity was at 254.634 s.
The blueprint score stayed at 47.619% and there were no recorded target changes in the 90-second window.
The handoff to Bob remained unresolved: Bob's claimed finishing role and available specialty blocks produced no observable task progress, while Andy's latest inventory contained only tools.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy attempted a detailed handoff but had no remaining observed building blocks and received no observable completion or progress from Bob.
Bob had gold and quartz stock and an existing role to finish the blueprint, yet showed no recorded action or material use during this window.

manager_advisory.message: Confirm Bob received Andy's handoff; have Bob immediately place the listed gold/quartz/glowstone fixes from his stock, while Andy runs the next blueprint check and reports only the remaining material gaps.

limitations: Target-change attribution was unavailable, so responsibility for any unrecorded world activity cannot be assigned.
Andy's action result was truncated and unclear; it may have continued beyond the shown API inspection.
This packet covers one 90-second window and does not establish whether either agent resumed work after the cutoff.

observations.claim: Andy assigned Bob all remaining coordinate placements via /setblock, but Andy recorded no execution commands or inventory changes during the window.
Bob continued gathering cobblestone and other materials after Andy's direct-placement handoff, with no confidently attributed target placement recorded.
The handoff did not produce observable blueprint progress: the score fell from 47.62% to 46.43%, and its last observed increase was 320 seconds before cutoff.
Andy appears to have usable capacity for coordinate placement but delegated the complete remaining scope to Bob, who was already handling collection and construction.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy issued a full-scope instruction to Bob but showed no task execution in this window despite previously identifying the required coordinate fixes.
Bob retained responsibility for collection and all remaining placements; observed activity was material gathering rather than completion of the new direct-placement handoff.

manager_advisory.message: Have Andy take half of the remaining coordinates and place them directly now; have Bob handle the other half, then run one blueprint check and divide any residual fixes.

limitations: No target block change was confidently attributed to either executor, so responsibility for the two correct-block removals is unknown.
The packet does not include the full latest blueprint-fix list or any blueprint-check output during this window.
Command telemetry may not capture every form of in-world action, although no positive score or attributed target progress was observed.

observations.claim: The structure reached a score of 100.0 by cutoff, with the last increase only 0.646 seconds earlier.
Andy’s check found only two remaining fixes, then Andy assigned Bob those exact stone-brick and quartz-block placements and requested verification.
Andy was confidently attributed 70 correct placements; the team recorded 90 correct placements overall, leaving 20 unattributed rather than attributable to Bob.
Bob had no recorded command or inventory change in this window, but his latest inventory contained both materials needed for the final assignment.
Although work was concentrated on Andy, the narrow final handoff matched Bob’s resources and the task completed by cutoff, so no unresolved or currently actionable imbalance remains.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy performed most confidently attributed finishing work, checked progress, and delegated the final two known fixes; this concentrated finishing phase ended in completion.
Bob had no recorded command in the window and possessed the final required materials, but 20 placements were unattributed and the score reached 100 after the handoff.

limitations: Only 77.8% of target changes were confidently attributed, so Bob’s contribution to the remaining 20 placements cannot be determined.
The packet contains no direct Bob telemetry after 405.009 seconds and no explicit response to Andy’s final assignment.
The window is terminal; it establishes successful completion but not which executor performed the last two fixes.
